title: Bacterial and Fungal DNA Extraction Protocol for Long-Read Whole Genome Sequencing
author: Manon Norest
materials: Fungal growth Place a fungal stab (obtained from the culture collection tube stored at 4°C) on four Inhibitory Mold Agar plates. Collect the mycelium from the four plates into a 5ml Eppendorf tube and incubate it at -80°C for 24h. Lyophilize the mycelium for 48h at -55°C. In this study we utilized the Heto PowerDry LL3000 freezedryer of ThermoScientific.

steps.title: Bacterial growth Bacteria growth can be...
steps.instruction: Bacterial growth Bacteria growth can be realized on Petri dishes (solid medium) or in a liquid medium (into 15ml tubes). Both techniques give high quality HMW bacterial DNA. To grow a bacterium into Petri dishes, streak the bacterial strain from the collection tube (normally placed at -80°C) onto Nutrient Agar plates with the addition of CaCl 2 . Incubate the bacteria at the appropriatetemperature. To grow a bacterium in liquid conditions, inoculate a single bacterial colony (previously obtained from a Nutrient Agar plate) into 10 mL of Nutrient Broth. Incubate the bacterial strain at appropriate temperature under constant agitation (120 rpm) until the stationary phase is reached.

steps.title: Fungal lysis Weigh 30 mg of...
steps.instruction: Fungal lysis Weigh 30 mg of dried mycelium and place it into the 2ml Eppendorf tube containing the glass beads. Add 800 µl of lysis buffer preheated at 65°C. Note : for some fungal strains, it is necessary to independenlty extract the DNA in several tubes. In this case, pool the independent DNA extractions in order to obtain a suitable amount of DNA. Mix the sample by using a Tissue Lyser at speed 6 m/s for 1 min and incubate at 65°C for 30 min.

steps.title: Bacterial lysis If growing bacteria in...
steps.instruction: Bacterial lysis If growing bacteria in Nutrient Broth, centrifuge the bacterial culture at 5000g for 10 min, discard the supernatant and resuspend the bacterial pellet in preheated 800 µL of lysis buffer by pipetting vigorously. If growing the bacteria on Nutrient Agar plates, pick few isolated bacterial colonies (take the equivalent of two toothpick tips) and mix them into the preheated 800 µl of lysis buffer. For both methods, transfer the obtained suspension into the 2ml Eppendorf tube containing the glass-beads. Mix the solution and incubate at 65°C for 30 min.

steps.title: Common steps to both bacteria and...
steps.instruction: Common steps to both bacteria and fungi - Add 60 µL of 20% SDS stock. - Homogenize the samples by using a Tissue Lyser at speed 6 m/s for 1 min. - Add 20 µl of lysozyme 100 mg/ml and incubate 30 min at 37°C. - Add 8 µl of proteinase K and incubate overnight at 56°C.

steps.title: Centrifuge 1 min at 10,000 x g
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 1 min at 10,000 x g.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to a new...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to a new tube (~ 650 µL) containing 200 µL of 5M potassium acetate.

steps.title: Mix and incubate on ice for 5 min
steps.instruction: Mix and incubate on ice for 5 min.

steps.title: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 2 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 2 min. Transfer the supernatant to a new tube (~ 500 µl).

steps.title: Add 350 µL of chloroform:Isoamyl alcohol (24:1)
steps.instruction: Add 350 µL of chloroform:Isoamyl alcohol (24:1).

steps.title: Invert the tubes several times until...
steps.instruction: Invert the tubes several times until the solution is homogenous and centrifuge 5 min at maximum speed.

steps.title: Take upper phase into a new...
steps.instruction: Take upper phase into a new tube (~ 300 µl) without disturb the inner phase. Note: if the upper phase contains traces of the microbial suspension, repeat the “chloroform:isoamyl alcohol” step twice .

steps.title: Mix the cleaned aqueous phase with...
steps.instruction: Mix the cleaned aqueous phase with an equal volume of isopropanol and a 1/8 volume of 3M sodium acetate. Note: DNA will precipitate and form “strings”.

steps.title: Incubate the samples on ice for 10 min
steps.instruction: Incubate the samples on ice for 10 min.

steps.title: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 5...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 5 min to obtain a DNA pellet.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant by reversing the...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant by reversing the tubes and let the samples dry for 5 min under the hood.

steps.title: Wash the pellet with 70% of...
steps.instruction: Wash the pellet with 70% of ethanol and add 600 µl of 70% ethanol, centrifuge 5 min at maximum speed, discard the supernatant by reversing tubes and let the samples dry for 5 min under the hood.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 200 µl...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 200 µl of Elution Buffer and add 900 µl of beads buffer. Note: This will purify DNA and remove small DNA fragments.

steps.title: Incubate on a rotator at 10...
steps.instruction: Incubate on a rotator at 10 rpm for 45 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Spin down the tubes for 1 second
steps.instruction: Spin down the tubes for 1 second.

steps.title: Place the tubes on a magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on a magnetic rack and wait for 5 min (until solution becomes clear).

steps.title: Discard the supernatant by pipetting without disturbing the beads
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant by pipetting without disturbing the beads.

steps.title: Add 70% ethanol to the tube. Note
steps.instruction: Add 70% ethanol to the tube. Note: Beads must be completely immersed in ethanol.

steps.title: Flick the tubes to resuspend beads,...
steps.instruction: Flick the tubes to resuspend beads, spin down and put the tubes back on the magnetic rack. Remove the ethanol without disturbing the beads.

steps.title: Repeat the steps 24 to 25...
steps.instruction: Repeat the steps 24 to 25 and remove the ethanol with a P200 / P10 pipette.

steps.title: Let the samples air drying on...
steps.instruction: Let the samples air drying on the magnetic rack for 5 min. Note : Beads must not dry too much otherwise resuspension can be difficult.

steps.title: Add 100 µl of Elution Buffer
steps.instruction: Add 100 µl of Elution Buffer.

steps.title: Resuspend the beads by gentling pipetting...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the beads by gentling pipetting and spin down the tube for 1 second. Note : Make sure the beads are not aggregated.

steps.title: Incubate the tubes for 15 min at 37°C
steps.instruction: Incubate the tubes for 15 min at 37°C.

steps.title: Spin down the tubes for 1 second
steps.instruction: Spin down the tubes for 1 second.

steps.title: Place the tubes on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on the magnetic rack and wait until the beads are well anchored and the solution is clear.

steps.title: Transfer the solution, consisting in the...
steps.instruction: Transfer the solution, consisting in the HMW DNA, into a 1.5ml Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Add 5 µl of 100 mg/ml RNase A
steps.instruction: Add 5 µl of 100 mg/ml RNase A.

steps.title: Incubate at 50°C for 15 min
steps.instruction: Incubate at 50°C for 15 min.

steps.title: Determine the DNA concentration with NanoDrop‱ or Qubit‱ Fluorometer
steps.instruction: Determine the DNA concentration with NanoDrop‱ or Qubit‱ Fluorometer. A 260/280 ratio should ideally be between 1.8 and 2. A 260/230 ratio should ideally be between 2 and 2.2.

steps.title: To check that DNA is not...
steps.instruction: To check that DNA is not degraded or contaminated with RNA, migrate the DNA samples on 1.5% agarose gel.

steps.title: Ladder
steps.instruction: Ladder : 100 pb, higher fragment = 1 kb A B C D E F 1 Gram-staining Bacterial species Number in the agarose gel DNA concentration ng/µl Quality OD260/280 Quality OD260/230 2 negative Sphingomonas sp 1 110 2.10 2.00 3 positive Actinobacteria sp 2 45 2.20 2.30 4 positive Arthrobacter sp 3 79 2.10 2.00 5 negative Pseudomonas moraviensis 4 173 2.20 2.00 6 positive Actinobacteria sp 5 35 2.00 1.70 7 negative Pseudomonas viridiflava 6 91 2.00 1.87 A B C D E 1 Fungal species Number in the agarose gel DNA concentration ng/µl Quality OD260/280 Quality OD260/230 2 Acremonium furcatum 7 178 1.76 0.89 3 Neopyrenochaeta inflorescentiae 8 738 2.02 1.55 4 Aureobasidium pullulans 9 1174 2.11 1.91 5 Metapochonia suchlasporia 10 362 2.05 1.91 6 uncultured fungus 11 862 2.04 1.96 7 Ascochyta rabiei 12 79 2.04 1.54 Nucleic acid quantifications was performed with Nanodrop TM . All DNAs have been successfully sequenced with the PacBio Sequel II HiFi technology.

steps.title: Before to start:i) Fill 2mL Eppendorf tubes with 0
steps.instruction: Before to start:i) Fill 2mL Eppendorf tubes with 0.5 mL of 1 mm glass beadsii) Heat the lysis buffer for ≥ 30 min at 65°C before use
